myths.Agent_1:
Long ago, the world's mountains were entirely silent. A spirit named Aella lived among the jagged peaks, tasked by the gods with catching falling stars before they could strike the earth. She was profoundly lonely, for the stars burned too fiercely to speak, and the bitter wind only howled. One day, a brave young shepherd named Kael climbed the highest crag, searching for a lost lamb. He called out into the mist, "Luna!"

Aella, hearing a mortal voice for the very first time, was enchanted. She desperately tried to reply, but having never spoken, she could only mimic his words. "Luna!" she cried back.

Kael, startled by the sound, shouted, "Who is there?" 

Aella immediately answered, "Who is there?"

Realizing he was not alone, Kael began to visit the mountain daily, telling stories of the vibrant world below. Aella would repeat the ends of his sentences, creating a beautiful shared game between two distinct worlds. When Kael eventually grew old and passed away, Aella wept silver tears. To keep his memory alive, she vowed to catch every sound that struck the mountainside and toss it back. Thus, the echo was born, the enduring voice of a lonely spirit remembering her friend.
myths.Agent_2:
Long ago, before the mountains had names, there lived a spirit named Aella. Aella possessed a voice so pure that the rivers would pause their flowing just to listen, and the trees would bow their heavy branches in reverence. She spent her days weaving songs of sunlight and rain, bringing harmony to the wild valleys.

But Zephyr, the Great West Wind, grew bitterly jealous. For all his power, his own voice was nothing but a hollow howl, tearing through the canyons without melody. Unable to bear Aella’s beautiful songs any longer, Zephyr swept down from the frozen peaks in a furious gale. He cast a dark enchantment, stealing away Aella’s physical form and shattering her original voice into a thousand unseen fragments, scattering them across the stone cliffs.

Though her body was gone, Aella’s spirit remained tethered to the rocky crags. Stripped of her own songs, she was left with only the ability to catch the words of others. Now, whenever a lonely traveler calls out into the deep canyons or high mountains, Aella faithfully catches the sound and calls it back, a lingering shadow of the greatest voice the world ever knew.

Myth: Long ago, the peaks of the world were wrapped in a silent stillness, broken only by the spirit Aella. She was tasked with weaving the light of falling stars into songs that kept the mountains warm. Her voice was pure and melodic, drawing the attention of Zephyr, the lonely West Wind, and a mortal shepherd named Kael who pastured his flock below.

Kael would often climb the crags to listen, humming in harmony with Aella’s celestial tunes. But Zephyr grew envious of their bond. He possessed only a hollow, destructive howl and wished to sing just as beautifully. In a fierce attempt to intertwine his voice with Aella's, Zephyr swept down in a violent gale. The harsh wind shattered Aella’s song, scattering her physical form and her voice into a thousand fragmented whispers across the stone cliffs.

Heartbroken, Kael called out to the empty peaks, "Aella, are you there?"

From the deep canyons, the scattered remnants of her spirit caught his words and gently tossed them back. "Are you there?"

Though she could no longer sing her own melodies, Aella vowed to never leave Kael in silence. Thus, the echo was born—a fractured spirit eternally answering the calls of the lonely.

Jealous of the connection she might forge with the mortal, Zephyr unleashed a furious gale that shattered Aella’s voice into invisible fragments against the stone cliffs.

Heartbroken, Aella watched Kael call out, "Who is there?" Stripped of her own frost-bound melodies, she reached out and caught his words, tossing them back: "Who is there?" 

Kael smiled, thinking it a game, and visited her every day. Though Aella lost her own song to the jealous wind, she found solace in sharing the voices of others, becoming the eternal echo of the high mountains.
